summary.text: Adds a k3s Kubernetes cluster to the distributed application.

All other cluster options are available as fluent builder methods:

agentCount.text: Number of k3s agent (worker) nodes to add. When
(the default) a single-node cluster is created — the server node acts as both control-plane and worker. Equivalent to calling

summary.text: Sets the number of k3s agent (worker) nodes to add to the cluster.

remarks.text: Agent nodes connect to the server via DCP DNS (
) and use k3s's built-in retry loop, so no explicit
is needed. The cluster health check waits for
to keep agents alive across AppHost restarts and avoid node password hash mismatches.

(single-node cluster — the server node acts as both control-plane and worker).

summary.text: Mounts a named Docker volume at the k3s data directory so cluster state persists across AppHost restarts.

remarks.text: , which contains the SQLite database, TLS certificates, and kubeconfig. Without this volume the cluster starts fresh on every AppHost launch. Combine with
on the cluster resource and its dependent Helm releases to avoid re-installing charts on every start.

members.name: WithK3sVersion
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<K3sClusterResource> K3sBuilderExtensions.WithK3sVersion(this IResourceBuilder<K3sClusterResource> builder, string tag)

summary.text: Sets the k3s image version used by the cluster server and all its agent nodes.

remarks.text: All agent nodes are immediately synced to the same tag to prevent version skew beyond the Kubernetes-supported ±1 minor version limit. The image tag is part of the DCP container identity, so synchronisation must happen at configuration time.

tag.text: The k3s container image tag, e.g.

members.name: WithLifetime
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<K3sClusterResource> K3sBuilderExtensions.WithLifetime(this IResourceBuilder<K3sClusterResource> builder, ContainerLifetime lifetime)

summary.text: Sets the container lifetime for the k3s cluster
and all its agent nodes

remarks.text: Agent nodes are propagated immediately because DCP uses
to compute container identity. Deferring propagation to

would be too late — DCP determines whether to reuse or recreate a persistent container before that event fires, so agents would lose their persistent identity and be recreated as new containers each run.

members.name: WithHelmValuesFile
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<HelmReleaseResource> K3sHelmBuilderExtensions.WithHelmValuesFile(this IResourceBuilder<HelmReleaseResource> builder, string path)

summary.text: Supplies a YAML values file to the Helm release (
--values

remarks.text: Use this method for structured overrides — particularly values containing commas, braces, or backslashes that cannot be safely expressed with

path.text: Path to the YAML values file on the host. Relative paths are resolved against the AppHost project directory. Call this method multiple times to supply additional files; they are applied in declaration order and later files win for duplicate keys.
